programmes.show_description: Nathan celebrates the best indie of the 2010s, with music from Tame Impala and HAIM.
Another chance to hear Mark Lamarr in full swing from September 2009... this time with The Heavy in session.
1/4. 6 Music Festival 2026 headliner Courtney Barnett shares her songwriting inspirations.
Want to hear the latest tracks 6 Music have added to their playlist? Then listen here!
Names in Songs returns, can you hear your name in a well-known song?
Wonderful music to start your day, including the Cloudbusting mix to banish the grey skies.
Film-makers Iain Forsyth and Jane Pollard join to talk about their new documentary film, Broken English, which is an exploration of the life and career of Marianne Faithfull.
Craig celebrates your midweek wins for Wednesday, I'm In Love, and we hear another of your left-field music suggestions as we turn Left At The Lights.
New discoveries from Huw and we get to know Ellen Beth Abdi in this week's 'Huw Are You'.
Witch Post chat to Nathan about the making of their new EP Butterfly, which is out on 20th March. The Scottish-American duo were formerly session guests and our Breaking Artist.
Marc Riley and Gideon Coe come together to play their music loves from every era and genre.
3/4. Tonight is all about Jacob's favourite queer artists that inspire them, and make them feel more possible, with a folk lean.
Swiss/French artist Noémi Büchi picks an hour of music.
Carlos Santana talks about his life and career. Presented by Alexis Korner. First broadcast in 1983.
Alexis Korner's 1983 profile of Scotty Moore, Elvis Presley's guitarist from his very first recordings on Sun Records until 1968. Scotty died in 2016.
2/4. 6 Music Festival 2026 headliner Courtney Barnett shares her songwriting inspirations.
Soundtrack the early hours with back-to-back music, plus exclusive BBC sessions.
Chris is joined by DJ and producer Jon Dasilva to talk about his new music and memories of the Madchester scene.
Comedian Josh Widdicombe chats to Nick about his favourite music and his new podcast, Museum of Pop Culture.
Anna Calvi joins ahead of the release of new collaborative EP, Is This All There Is? and also shares her musical DNA.
Comedians Flo & Joan stop by in Salford to chat to Craig all about their upcoming stand-up tour 'With Feeling'.
Huw has handpicked music, old and new, plus we round up the gigs, shows and festivals that everybody is talking about in today's Gig Lisztomania.
Reviewing this week's new releases.
Start your weekend early with an exclusive guest mix from Manchester-based DJ Warpfit, who is known for blending bassline and speed garage.
Gideon Coe with his music loves from every era and genre. Expect archive sifting and new tunes galore.
4/4. Jacob explores songs that evoke the feeling of Limerence, and music that inspired their debut album 'In Limerence'.
